sections.paragraph:
- Original: Ἰαννά

- Transliteration: Ianna

- Phonetic: ee-an-nah'

- Definition: Janna = "flourishing"

1. son of Joseph and the father of Melchi, in the genealogy of Christ

- Origin: probably of hebrew origin, cf H3238

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Probably of hebrew origin (compare [H3238]); Janna an Israelite: - Janna.
